rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place component type annexes in the component type scope and implementation annexes in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or explicitly imported namespaces
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by inserting annex blocks in the correct scope
Preserve existing connections and subcomponents when adding new annex blocks
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or explicitly imported namespaces
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees that refer to subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not assume a type for an expression that differs from the actual type of the expression
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax details not directly related to annex placement
